figures.caption: Fig. 3: Mid-tree void nucleation in the Dual-Port RAM design. Left: stress profile along tree (25-100) over outer timesteps from 2.5 × 106 s to 5.0 × 107 s, showing the evolution from a near-zero state to a strong compressive gradient as degradation accumulates. Right: stress history at junction node 220, where the abrupt transition from about 3.3× 108 Pa tensile stress to about −3.4× 108 Pa compressive stress marks void nucleation; the subsequent saturation confirms that the Robin boundary condition enforces flux decoupling at the interior node without dynamic tree splitting.
Fig. 7: Power grid structure of the ARM Cortex-A logic core (three metal layers M1, M8, M9 shown together with VIA connections)
Fig. 6: Temperature maps, stress distributions, and IR drop maps for the RISC-V core under two thermal scenarios
Fig. 4: RISC-V core: finished physical layout (left) and extracted power grid structure showing four metal layers (M1, M2, M7, M8) with VIA connections (right).

TABLE III: Performance comparison of EMSpice 3 across benchmark designs. A TTF entry of > 5.0 × 107 s indicates that the 10% IR-drop failure threshold was not reached within the simulated time window. The Krylov metric error is measured on TTF when failure occurs, and on the final IR drop otherwise.
Fig. 8: Temperature maps, stress distributions, and IR drop maps for the ARM Cortex-A logic core under two thermal scenarios
